folio.label: 178v
folio.l:
& bigy~netú æe Monkes tale , æ is
titled de casibus virorum Illustrium ; Chaucer
I wol be wayle , in manere of tragedetragedietragedie
The harm of hem , æ stoden in hey degree
And fellen so , æ æäe was no remedie
To brynge he~ out , of here aduäsitee
For certeyn , whan æ fortune list to flee
There may noman , the cours of hire witú holde
Lat noman truste , on blynd ¸s¹itee
Be war , by theise ensamples , trewe & olde
¢ At lucifer , thougú he an Aungel were
And nat a man , at úi~ I wol bygynne
For thougú fortune may , non Aungel dere
From heigú degree , ©et fel he for his synne
Doun in to helle , where as he ©et is Inne
O lucifer brigútest , of Aungelis alle
Now artow Sathanas , æ maist nat twynne
Out of miserie , in whicú æ æè art falle
¢ Loo Adam , in the feld of Damyssene
W goddes owne fynger , wrougút was he
And nat bigeten , of ma~nes s¹me vnclene
And welte al Paradys , sauynge o tree
Hadde neuäe wordly man , so heigú degree
As Adam , til he for mysgouänaunce
Was dryuen out , of his heye ¸s¹itee
To labour & to helle , and to meschaunce
¢ Loo Sampsou~ , whicú æ was , Anunciat
By the Aungel , longe , or his natyuyte
And was to god almygúty , consecrat
And stod in noblesse , whil he mygút se
Was neuäe sucú a nother , as was he
To speke of strengthe , & ther w hardynesse
But to hise wyues , told he his secree
